nodes.title: Lost Lineage of Human Knowledge Augmentation
nodes.summary: A parallel history of computing tried to preserve human understanding as connected, navigable, attributable knowledge.

nodes.title: Paul Otlet
nodes.summary: Documentalist associated with the Mundaneum, universal bibliography, and early visions of networked document access.

nodes.title: H. G. Wells
nodes.summary: Writer and futurist who proposed a World Brain as a planetary knowledge memory.

nodes.title: Douglas Adams
nodes.summary: Writer and technologist who popularized the fundamental interconnectedness of things and introduced Hyperland to a broad audience.

nodes.title: Hyperland
nodes.summary: A 1990 BBC hypermedia documentary by Douglas Adams that dramatized software agents, hypertext, Xanadu, micons, and associative browsing.

nodes.title: Tom Baker
nodes.summary: Actor who appears in Hyperland as a software-agent persona guiding knowledge navigation.

nodes.title: Max Whitby
nodes.summary: Producer and director associated with Hyperland.

nodes.title: TED2
nodes.summary: The February 1990 TED conference in Monterey where hypertext, interactive media, virtual reality, design, and knowledge-navigation ideas converged.

nodes.title: Monterey, California
nodes.summary: The site of TED2 in February 1990.

nodes.title: Bob Stein
nodes.summary: Interactive publishing pioneer associated with Criterion, Voyager, and early electronic books.

nodes.title: Voyager Company
nodes.summary: Interactive publishing company in the laserdisc and CD-ROM lineage.

nodes.title: Alan Kay
nodes.summary: Computing pioneer associated with Dynabook, Smalltalk, personal dynamic media, and computers as tools for improving thought.

nodes.title: Xerox PARC
nodes.summary: Research center associated with GUI, Smalltalk, personal computing, and dynamic media.

nodes.title: HyperCard
nodes.summary: Apple hypermedia authoring system that popularized personal card-based linking and scripting.

nodes.title: Tim Berners-Lee
nodes.summary: Inventor of the World Wide Web, a simpler distributed hypertext branch that solved broad publishing and retrieval.

nodes.title: World Wide Web
nodes.summary: A distributed hypertext system that made publishing easy while leaving many provenance, transclusion, and semantic relationship problems unsolved.

nodes.title: Aspen Movie Map
nodes.summary: An early navigable spatial media project often treated as part of the hypermedia and virtual environment lineage.

nodes.title: Lost Lineage Intertwingled Trail
nodes.summary: A JSON-imported trail showing that the lineage is an interwoven graph, not a single heroic family tree.
